---
title: "Economics Dashboard"
format: 
  dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)

library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(dplyr)
```

## Row

## Introduction
Hello

## Row 
### Main chart (required)

```{r}
df <- read.csv("StudentPerformanceFactors.csv")

#rename columns
df <- df %>%
  rename(
     HoursStudied = Hours_Studied,
     ExamScore = Exam_Score,
     Attendance = Attendance
   )

#filter outliers
q1_h <- quantile(df$HoursStudied, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
q3_h <- quantile(df$HoursStudied, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
iqr_h <- q3_h - q1_h

q1_s <- quantile(df$ExamScore, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
q3_s <- quantile(df$ExamScore, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
iqr_s <- q3_s - q1_s

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    is_outlier =
      HoursStudied < q1_h - 1.5 * iqr_h |
      HoursStudied > q3_h + 1.5 * iqr_h |
      ExamScore < q1_s - 1.5 * iqr_s |
      ExamScore > q3_s + 1.5 * iqr_s
  )

df_main <- df %>% filter(!is_outlier)
df_out  <- df %>% filter(is_outlier)


#fit regression line
fit <- lm(ExamScore ~ HoursStudied, data = df_main)

slope <- coef(fit)[2]
intercept <- coef(fit)[1]
corr <- cor(df_main$HoursStudied, df_main$ExamScore, use = "complete.obs")
r2 <- summary(fit)$r.squared

# Hover text
df_main <- df_main %>%
  mutate(
    point_text = paste0(
      "Hours Studied: ", HoursStudied,
      "<br>Exam Score: ", ExamScore,
      "<br>Attendance: ", Attendance,
      "<br>Status: Non-outlier"
    )
  )

df_out <- df_out %>%
  mutate(
    point_text = paste0(
      "Hours Studied: ", HoursStudied,
      "<br>Exam Score: ", ExamScore,
      "<br>Attendance: ", Attendance,
      "<br>Status: Outlier"
    )
  )

# Predicted values for the regression line
x_seq <- seq(
  min(df_main$HoursStudied, na.rm = TRUE),
  max(df_main$HoursStudied, na.rm = TRUE),
  length.out = 200
)

line_df <- data.frame(
  HoursStudied = x_seq,
  ExamScore = predict(fit, newdata = data.frame(HoursStudied = x_seq))
)

line_text <- paste0(
  "Trend line",
  "<br>Slope: ", round(slope, 3),
  "<br>Intercept: ", round(intercept, 3),
  "<br>Correlation (r): ", round(corr, 3),
  "<br>R²: ", round(r2, 3)
)

p <- plot_ly()

p <- plot_ly()

# NON-OUTLIERS
p <- p %>%
  add_markers(
    data = df_main,
    x = ~HoursStudied,
    y = ~ExamScore,
    color = ~Attendance,
    colors = c("#ffffcc", "#006400"),
    text = ~point_text,
    hoverinfo = "text",
    marker = list(
      symbol = "circle-open",
      size = 8,
      line = list(width = 1.2)
    ),
    name = "Data (no outliers)"
  )

# OUTLIERS (same look!)
p <- p %>%
  add_markers(
    data = df_out,
    x = ~HoursStudied,
    y = ~ExamScore,
    color = ~Attendance,
    colors = c("#ffffcc", "#006400"),
    text = ~point_text,
    hoverinfo = "text",
    marker = list(
      symbol = "circle-open",   # same shape
      size = 8,
      line = list(width = 1.2)
    ),
    name = "Outliers"
  )

# TREND LINE
p <- p %>%
  add_lines(
    data = line_df,
    x = ~HoursStudied,
    y = ~ExamScore,
    text = line_text,
    hoverinfo = "text",
    line = list(color = "black", width = 2),
    name = "Trend line"
  )

# Layout
p %>%
  layout(
    title = "Exam Score vs Hours Studied",
    xaxis = list(title = "Hours Studied"),
    yaxis = list(title = "Exam Score"),
    dragmode = "zoom",
    legend = list(title = list(text = "Click to toggle"))
  ) %>%
  config(
    displayModeBar = TRUE,
    scrollZoom = TRUE
  )

```
